NCT04423718.name: PULSAR
NCT04423718.group: Treatment-naive nAMD, aflibercept 8 mg IVT Q12W/Q16W vs aflibercept 2 mg Q8W (wk 48, BCVA MD)

allOutcomes.title: BCVA letter-score change at week 48 (primary, 8 mg Q12W minus 2 mg Q8W, MD)
Aflibercept 8 mg patients maintaining Q12W dosing without rescue at week 48 (proportion, descriptive)
Aflibercept 8 mg patients achieving Q16W dosing extension at week 48 (proportion, descriptive)
Central subfield thickness change at week 48 (8 mg minus 2 mg; um, MD; secondary)

NCT04423718.snippet: Source: Lanzetta P et al. Lancet 2024;403:1153-1163 (PULSAR 48-wk).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,009 adults with treatment-naive neovascular age-related macular degeneration randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to aflibercept 8 mg IVT every 12 weeks (n=335), aflibercept 8 mg every 16 weeks (n=338), or aflibercept 2 mg every 8 weeks (n=336), all after a 3-dose monthly loading phase, for 48 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - BCVA at Week 48 (non-inferiority)
evidence.text: Adjusted mean change in BCVA letter score from baseline at week 48: aflibercept 8 mg Q12W +6.1 letters vs 2 mg Q8W +5.1 letters; estimated treatment difference +1.0 letter (95% CI -0.4 to +2.5). Non-inferiority margin of -4 letters met (primary endpoint).

evidence.label: Secondary - Dosing Interval Achievement
evidence.text: 77% of 8 mg Q12W patients maintained Q12W dosing through week 48 without need for interval shortening; 79% of 8 mg Q16W patients maintained Q16W. Safety balanced across arms; no new IOP/inflammation signals.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind with matched syringe volume, masked BCVA examiners. D3 LOW - 94% completion. D4 LOW - central reading center. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Active subfoveal CNV secondary to nAMD, including juxtafoveal lesions that affect the fovea as assessed in the study eye.~* Total area of CNV (including both classic and occult components) must comprise greater than 50% of the total lesion area in the study eye.~* BCVA ETDRS letter score of 78 to 24 (corresponding to a Snellen equivalent of approximately 20/32 to 20/320) in the study eye.~* Decrease in BCVA determined to be primarily the result of nAMD in the study eye.~* ...

NCT04429503.name: PHOTON
NCT04429503.group: DME, aflibercept 8 mg IVT Q12W/Q16W vs aflibercept 2 mg Q8W (wk 48, BCVA MD)

allOutcomes.title: BCVA letter-score change at week 48 (primary, 8 mg Q12W minus 2 mg Q8W, MD)
Aflibercept 8 mg patients maintaining Q12W dosing without rescue at week 48 (proportion, descriptive)
Aflibercept 8 mg patients achieving Q16W dosing extension at week 48 (proportion, descriptive)
Central subfield thickness change at week 48 (8 mg minus 2 mg; um, MD; secondary)

NCT04429503.snippet: Source: Brown DM et al. Lancet 2024;403:1226-1237 (PHOTON).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 658 adults with diabetic macular edema and visual-acuity impairment randomized 1:2:1 double-blind to aflibercept 2 mg Q8W (n=167), aflibercept 8 mg Q12W (n=328), or aflibercept 8 mg Q16W (n=163), for 48 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - BCVA at Week 48 (non-inferiority)
evidence.text: Adjusted mean BCVA change at week 48: aflibercept 8 mg Q12W +8.1 letters vs 2 mg Q8W +7.7 letters; estimated treatment difference +0.5 letter (95% CI -0.9 to +1.9). Non-inferiority margin of -4 letters met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Dosing Interval and Retinal Anatomy
evidence.text: 89% of 8 mg Q12W patients maintained Q12W; 83% of 8 mg Q16W maintained Q16W. Central subfield thickness reduction comparable to 2 mg Q8W. No new safety signals.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind with masked BCVA examiners. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - central reading center. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Diabetic macular edema (DME) with central involvement in the study eye~* Best corrected visual acuity (BCVA) early treatment diabetic retinopathy study (ETDRS) letter score of 78 to 24 (approximate Snellen equivalent of 20/32 to 20/320) in the study eye with decreased vision determined to be primarily the result of DME~* Willing and able to comply with clinic visits and study-related procedures~* Provide informed consent signed by study participant or legally acceptabl...

title: RapidMeta Ophthalmology | Aflibercept 8 mg High-Dose for nAMD and DME v1.1
